be: in the first Purusa (5 cubits) a white and black snake of one
cubit length; to its south flows a vein with plenty of brackish water.
Should there be an ant-hill to the north of a Karrra (Garil)
tree, there would be sweet water 50 cubits below at a distance of 4-5
cubits to the south of the tree. The sign at a depth of 5 cubits is a
yellow frog.
[The word Karira should not be taken here to mean 'the
tender bamboo shoot.' For, it is a desert tree, Maru vrksah, says
Kslrasvamin (Amara 11-77). He says (in 111-1740 it is or a
variety of bamboo.

Exploration of Water Springs LIV 513
It" there be an ant-hill to the west of a Rohiiaka tree, there
would be a westerly vein of brackish water 60-cubits underneath at
a distance of 3 cubits to the south of the tree.
[According to the Amarakosa the tree is Rohitaka. It is other-
wise known as ki) ^—-enemy of spleen ]
